names.thesis: Materion is a specialty materials supplier to semiconductors/aerospace/defense/energy, not a pure LME-aluminum play, so it largely sidesteps our sector-level Aluminum SHORT bias. Q1 2026 results showed double-digit growth across most end markets, record backlog, and reiterated $6.00-6.50 adjusted EPS guidance with confidence toward the high end, driving a 28% four-week share rally and a 14th straight annual dividend hike. Near-term catalyst is the July 9 New Ideas Summer Investor Conference presentation; customer concentration in semis/aerospace/defense remains the key risk.
catalysts: Russell 2000 Dynamic Index reclassification flows
July 9 New Ideas Summer Investor Conference presentation
Continued semiconductor/aerospace/defense order momentum
risks: Customer concentration in cyclical semiconductor end market
Broader Aluminum SHORT backdrop if input-cost dynamics shift
Valuation re-rating after 28% 4-week run

analyst.note: No explicit sell-side rating in evidence; stock cited for 4.2% single-day jump on volume, 28.1% 4-week gain

evidence.note: MTRN +4.2% on upbeat Q1 results, record backlog, reiterated $6.00-6.50 adj. EPS guidance for FY2026
Russell 2000 Dynamic Index reclassification; management to present at July 9 investor conference on semiconductor/aerospace/energy exposure
Aluminum industry sector view: electrolytic aluminum capacity nearing ceiling, aluminum prices expected to enter upward cycle medium/long term

names.thesis: CSN (National Steel) posted Q1 2026 consolidated EBITDA up ~5.5-5.6% YoY with 1.8pt margin expansion, helped by diversification into cement and logistics offsetting weaker steel/mining, and reduced leverage to 3.36x from 3.47x. This supports our Steel LONG bias, but Moody's-linked commentary flags leverage staying elevated (5.5x-6.5x over 12-18 months) on lower steel and iron ore prices, with 2026 iron ore production/shipments expected roughly flat to down ~1.5Mt in H1, capping near-term upside absent asset sales or accelerated deleveraging.
catalysts: Further deleveraging via debt reduction or asset sales
Iron ore price recovery toward $80-100/t (61% Fe) scenario
Continued cement/logistics diversification offsetting steel weakness
risks: Elevated leverage (5.8x Moody's-adjusted) limits credit upgrade path
Steel import pressure and BRL strength cited as 2026 headwinds
Flat-to-declining iron ore shipments in H1 2026

analyst.note: Moody's-linked credit commentary: leverage expected to remain 5.5x-6.5x over next 12-18 months absent deleveraging action

evidence.note: Q1 2026 EBITDA +5.5-5.6% YoY, leverage down to 3.36x from 3.47x, diversified portfolio credited
Adjusted EBITDA ~BRL8.8bn TTM to March 2026, leverage 5.8x; expected to stay 5.5x-6.5x on lower steel/iron ore prices
CSN 2026 iron ore production seen flat vs 2025; H1 2026 shipments to fall ~1.5Mt

names.thesis: Ero Copper fits squarely in our Copper LONG bucket: consensus 2026 EPS growth of 93.9% with estimates revised up 7.3% over the past 60 days, a Zacks Rank #2 and Value Score A, and continued de-risking of the Furnas Copper-Gold project (75,000+ meters drilled, deposit still open, pre-feasibility study targeted 2027) that extends the long-term copper growth runway. Q2 2026 operating/financial results are due August 5, 2026, providing a near-term catalyst.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results release August 5, 2026
Furnas pre-feasibility study progress toward 2027
Continued positive earnings estimate revisions
risks: Brazil operating/regulatory/FX risk across Caraíba, Tucumã, Xavantina
Furnas remains development-stage with permitting/engineering risk
Copper price volatility despite structural electrification demand

analyst.note: Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), Value Score A; 2026 consensus EPS growth 93.9%, estimate revised +7.3% over 60 days

evidence.note: Furnas drilling update: 75,000+ meters completed, 10 rigs active, pre-feasibility study expected 2027
ERO Zacks Rank #2, Value Score A, 2026 EPS growth estimate 93.9%, revised up 7.3% in 60 days
Q2 2026 operating and financial results scheduled August 5, 2026

names.thesis: Sandfire Resources reinforces our Copper LONG thesis with a materially upgraded Black Butte (Montana) pre-feasibility study: total ore reserves up 49% to 14.3Mt at 2.6% Cu, mine life extended ~50% to 12 years, and a maiden Lowry reserve of 100,000t contained copper on a conservative long-term copper price assumption of $4.70/lb. Shares rose 4% on the news. The June 2026 quarterly report (July 23) is the next catalyst for group-wide production and cost trends.
catalysts: June 2026 Quarterly Report and investor teleconference on July 23, 2026
Black Butte project advancing through permitting (Johnny Lee fully permitted, Lowry in approvals)
Continued resource growth at Lowry deposit, still open
risks: US permitting timeline for Lowry deposit
Initial capex of ~$474M plus $180M sustaining capex at Black Butte
General copper price volatility vs. PFS assumption of $4.70/lb

analyst.note: No explicit rating cited in evidence; market reacted positively (+4%) to PFS update

evidence.note: Black Butte PFS: reserves +49% to 14.3Mt at 2.6% Cu, mine life +50% to 12 years, shares +4%
June 2026 quarterly report due July 23 with investor teleconference; production/cost focus

names.thesis: GE Vernova is primarily an AI/grid electrification infrastructure story rather than a direct electrical-steel price play; Q1 2026 revenue grew 17% YoY to $9.34B with an EPS beat, and management raised full-year 2026 guidance (revenue to $44.5-45.5B, adjusted EBITDA margin to 12-14%, free cash flow to $6.5-7.5B) on strong Power and Electrification orders/backlog. As a buyer rather than producer of electrical steel, our sector Steel LONG bias is a mild input-cost headwind rather than a tailwind for GEV's margins, but multi-year turbine/grid order visibility and Bernstein's Outperform initiation (PT $1,206) support the name on its own AI-power-demand merits.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results July 22, 2026
Continued backlog growth in Power and Electrification segments
Grid/AI power demand driving multi-year equipment orders
risks: Premium valuation flagged by sell-side commentary
Electrical steel and other input cost inflation given Steel LONG backdrop
Execution risk on raised FY2026 guidance

analyst.note: Bernstein: Outperform initiated June 17, 2026, PT $1,206 (~8%+ upside)

evidence.note: Q1 2026 revenue $9.34B (+17% YoY), EPS beat by $1.95, FY2026 guidance raised across metrics
FY2026 guidance raised: revenue $44.5-45.5B, adj. EBITDA margin 12-14%, FCF $6.5-7.5B
Bernstein initiates Outperform, PT $1,206, citing energy security/decarbonization demand

names.thesis: GLD tracks spot gold, which has pulled back sharply from its January 2026 all-time high above $5,300/oz to around $4,100 by July 1 - a fourth consecutive monthly decline including a 10% drop in June - consistent with our gold SHORT-medium bias even as central banks remain structural buyers (474t purchased in Q1 2026, second-highest quarterly total on record, with ~90% of central banks planning further additions). The 12-month total return of +22.27% masks a deteriorating recent trend that argues for caution on fresh long exposure at these levels.
catalysts: 10-year TIPS real yield trajectory (currently 2.26%) as a swing factor if Fed easing resumes
Central bank buying providing a longer-term floor
Gold-silver ratio dynamics
risks: Continued near-term downside momentum (4 straight monthly declines)
Real yields backing up further, pressuring non-yielding bullion
Reversal risk if safe-haven demand resurges on macro shocks

analyst.note: Sell-side desks described as 'rebuilding commodity assumptions' after the 12-month rally; no single house rating cited

evidence.note: Gold fell from ATH >$5,300/oz (Jan 2026) to ~$4,100 by July 1, 4th straight monthly decline, -10% in June
GLD +22.27% trailing 12-month total return; watch 10Y TIPS real yield at 2.26% for next leg
GLD expense ratio 0.40%, tracks physical gold bullion, share price $378.13 as of July 2, 2026

names.thesis: Aqua Metals is a small-cap battery-materials recycler rather than a primary manganese producer; it validated 99.8% purity manganese sulfate production alongside battery-grade lithium carbonate at its Innovation Center, and management cites improving 2026 lithium/battery-materials market conditions supporting renewed commercialization push. However, the balance sheet remains thin ($7.5M working capital), the company continues to post multi-year net losses (~$22.6M in 2025), and it carries incremental credit exposure (~$4.1M) to Lion Energy financing - keeping this a speculative, capital-constrained story consistent with a neutral manganese stance rather than a clear directional call.
catalysts: Continued scale-up of AquaRefining platform toward commercial deployment
2026 Annual Meeting August 18, 2026
Improving lithium/battery materials market conditions
risks: Thin liquidity ($7.5M working capital) relative to commercialization capex needs
Lion Energy financing exposure (~$4.1M)
Continued net losses; phased/capital-efficient approach implies slow scale-up

analyst.note: No sell-side rating in evidence; company self-describes 'position of resilience and strategic readiness' entering commercialization phase

evidence.note: $7.5M working capital; manganese sulfate purity ~99.8% achieved; Lion Energy exposure ~$4.1M
Lithium/battery materials market improving in 2026; phased, capital-efficient commercialization approach
Net loss $(22.6)M in 2025 vs $(24.6)M in 2024; TSR declined 8% 2024-2025

names.thesis: Firebird Metals is an early-stage manganese developer; the only recent evidence found is an AU$2 million Australian government grant to build the country's first demonstration-scale facility converting manganese concentrate into battery cathode materials in Perth, aimed at manganese-rich battery demand. This is a modest positive de-risking step but far too early-stage to move off a neutral manganese stance, and the evidence base from the last two months is very thin.
catalysts: Construction/commissioning of the Perth demonstration-scale cathode materials facility
Progress toward manganese-rich battery supply chain positioning
risks: Very early-stage, pre-revenue development company
Thin news/research coverage limits visibility into near-term catalysts
Broader manganese price/demand remains neutral per sector view

analyst.note: No sell-side coverage found in evidence window

evidence.note: Firebird Metals received AU$2M grant to develop Australia's first demonstration-scale manganese concentrate-to-cathode-materials facility in Perth

names.thesis: Anglo American continues its portfolio simplification into a copper-centric miner - Teck merger overwhelmingly shareholder-approved and expected to close between September 2026 and March 2027, giving 'more than 70% exposure to copper,' with the Anglo Platinum (Valterra) demerger and nickel/diamond asset sales completed and unchanged 2026 copper guidance of 700-760kt reiterated. This supports our Copper LONG bucket, but Berenberg downgraded to Hold ahead of H1 results (due July 23/30) on expectations of a slight consensus miss ($9.8B revenue/$0.63 EPS vs. $9.9B/$0.78 consensus), and a Chilean tribunal ruling threatens the Collahuasi copper project's environmental authorization.
catalysts: Q2 2026 production report (July 23) and H1 2026 financial results (July 30)
Teck merger completion expected Q4 2026-Q1 2027, final hurdle being Chinese antitrust approval
Australian steelmaking coal sale to Dhilmar (up to $3.88B) simplifying the portfolio
risks: Berenberg sees scope for H1 consensus disappointment from underperforming non-core assets
Collahuasi environmental authorization challenge in Chile (no immediate production impact currently)
Merger execution/regulatory risk on Teck combination timeline

analyst.note: Berenberg: downgraded to Hold from Buy on June 18, 2026, PT unchanged at £42; separately reiterated Buy in May on coal-sale-driven model update

evidence.note: Berenberg downgrades to Hold, PT £42, flags H1 consensus miss risk; H1 results July 23/30
Q1 2026 copper production 170,400t; unchanged 2026 guidance 700-760kt copper; Collahuasi environmental ruling
Australian coal asset sale to Dhilmar up to $3.88B; Teck merger cited as catalyst

names.thesis: Eramet remains consistent with our Nickel NEUTRAL stance: nickel prices near $19,000/t are supportive, but the company hasn't reached full nameplate capacity at its Argentina nickel site and its Indonesian nickel ore mining permits are now exhausted, limiting its ability to capitalize on H2 strength, while manganese prices are softening after a strong start to the year. Oddo BHF maintained a Neutral rating (PT raised to €63) but flagged the stock as 'largely uninvestable' pending clarity on a €500M capital increase, high leverage (ND/EBITDA >5x), and governance uncertainty (interim CEO, rumored Duval family exit).
catalysts: H1 2026 results July 29, 2026
Senegal Grande Côte mineral sands restart progressing toward 100% nameplate capacity by Q1 2027
Resolution of €500M capital increase and CEO/governance overhang
risks: ND/EBITDA leverage above 5x
Governance uncertainty (interim CEO, capital increase terms unresolved)
Nickel ore permit exhaustion in Indonesia capping upside capture

analyst.note: Oddo BHF: Neutral, PT raised to €63 from €61 on May 28, 2026

evidence.note: Senegal Grande Côte HMC production restarted at 30% of nameplate; 2026 guidance reinstated at 300-400kt

Oddo BHF Neutral, PT €63; nickel ~$19,000/t and lithium ~$25,000/t supportive but Argentina/Indonesia nickel capacity constraints; manganese softening; stock 'largely uninvestable' pending capital increase clarity

names.thesis: Anglo American Platinum (now demerged as Valterra Platinum) is our highest-conviction PGM short, but recent evidence cuts against that stance near-term: Berenberg upgraded Valterra to Buy from Hold on June 19, 2026, raising price targets to £71/R1,550, calling shareholder returns an 'immediate priority' and describing itself as 'constructive on the PGM outlook.' We flag this as a genuine risk to the short thesis and will watch the Q2 production report (July 17) and H1 results (July 29) closely, alongside Berenberg's forecast of H1 revenue and EBITDA below Visible Alpha consensus (ZAR71.6bn vs ZAR78.2bn revenue).
catalysts: Q2 2026 production results July 17, 2026
H1 2026 interim results July 29, 2026
Potential capital returns given company's 'immediate priority' framing per Berenberg
risks: Berenberg upgrade and constructive PGM outlook commentary directly conflict with our SHORT-high positioning
Production shifted to Q3 to manage winter power levies could distort H1/Q3 comparisons
Berenberg's own H1 estimates sit below consensus, suggesting downside surprise risk despite the upgrade

analyst.note: Berenberg: upgraded to Buy from Hold on June 19, 2026, PT raised to £71 (LSE) / R1,550 (JSE); H1 estimates below Visible Alpha consensus

evidence.note: Berenberg upgrades Valterra Platinum to Buy, PT £71/R1,550, cites constructive PGM outlook and shareholder-return priority; Q2 production July 17, H1 results July 29
Anglo Platinum demerger to Valterra Platinum completed May 2025, part of Anglo's copper-focused simplification post-Teck merger approval

names.thesis: Evolution Metals & Technologies (recently S-1 filer, Korea-based operations) fits our Rare Earths LONG thesis structurally - it is expanding capacity for battery metals and rare earth elements, and expects that any China-driven increase in rare earth procurement costs would likely be passed through to customers given RE's strategic-material status for US/European defense and industry. However, standalone financials are weak: FY2025 revenue fell 6.1% to $458M with an operating margin that deteriorated to -89.6%, and the company remains exposed to KRW/USD FX and broad commodity-price volatility.
catalysts: S-1 registration/listing process progressing (filed July 8, 2026)
Capacity expansion in battery metals and rare earth processing
Potential pass-through pricing power if China tightens rare earth supply
risks: Widening operating losses (-89.6% margin in 2025)
Revenue decline (-6.1% YoY) on fewer completed projects
KRW/USD FX exposure given Korea-based operations; unquantified trade-policy risk

analyst.note: No sell-side rating found; company is in S-1 registration process

evidence.note: FY2025 revenue $458M (-6.1% YoY), operating margin -89.6%; expanding capacity for battery metals/rare earth elements
Rare earths viewed as strategic/critical materials for US defense and European economies; cost increases expected to be passed through over time

names.thesis: Silvercorp delivered a strong Fiscal 2026 (adjusted net income $151M, record $310.6M operating cash flow, cash cost per ounce of silver net of by-product credits at negative $0.01), consistent with efficient low-cost operations. However, new nationwide Chinese mining safety measures - triggered by a Shanxi coal mine accident - will cut Ying Mining District output by 40-50% and GC mine output by ~50% in the July-September quarter, a meaningful near-term production headwind. Combined with the broader silver price pullback noted alongside gold's decline, this supports a cautious, SHORT-low stance rather than chasing the name here.
catalysts: Resolution of new safety compliance work (~$5.5M cost, ~50 days) at Ying and GC
Silver price recovery if industrial/investment demand returns to a structurally deficit market
Continued low-cost production once safety upgrades complete
risks: 40-50% production cut at Ying, ~50% at GC through Q3 (Jul-Sep) quarter from new China mining safety regulations
Broader silver spot price weakness (4th straight monthly decline alongside gold in June)
Regulatory/political risk tied to China-wide mining safety crackdown

analyst.note: No explicit sell-side rating in evidence; Q4 FY2026 adjusted EPS $0.27 beat consensus $0.25, revenue $147.4M beat $144.1M consensus

evidence.note: FY2026 adjusted net income $151M ($0.69/sh), record operating cash flow $310.6M, cash cost per oz silver net of by-products -$0.01
New China mining safety measures to cut Ying output 40-50% and GC output ~50% in Jul-Sep quarter
Fiscal Q4 adjusted EPS $0.27 beat $0.25 consensus; revenue $147.4M beat $144.1M consensus

names.thesis: MMG Limited (HKG:1208), the Hong Kong-listed base-metals miner grouped in our Zinc/Lead/Tin long bucket, is raising significant growth capital: a HK$6.25 billion share placement (705.9M shares at HK$8.88, 5.5% of enlarged capital) plus $800M of zero-coupon convertible bonds due 2027, explicitly to refinance loans and fund development/expansion of existing projects. This signals management confidence in deploying capital into project growth during a period of base-metals strength, consistent with a constructive stance, though the placement and convertible issuance also carry near-term dilution.
catalysts: Deployment of placement/convertible proceeds into project development and expansion
Continued strength in copper/base metals pricing supporting project economics
risks: ~10%+ potential share dilution from placement plus convertible bond conversion
Execution risk on expansion project pipeline
General base-metals price volatility

analyst.note: No explicit sell-side rating in evidence for this window

evidence.note: MMG (1208.HK) raising HK$6.25bn via share placement plus $800M zero-coupon convertible bonds due 2027 to refinance loans and fund project development/expansion

names.thesis: Straits Trading's tin exposure via subsidiary Malaysia Smelting Corporation (MSC) strongly supports our Zinc/Tin LONG thesis: MSC's Q1 FY2026 net profit surged more than fivefold to RM42.93M (highest since 1QFY2022) on a 23.6% revenue increase, driven by average tin prices up nearly 36% YoY (RM193,100/tonne vs RM142,000/tonne) and stronger mining and smelting performance. MSC declared a special dividend of 4 sen/share, underscoring cash generation strength flowing through to the Straits Trading parent.
catalysts: Continued elevated tin pricing flowing through MSC's mining and smelting segments
Special dividend payment (June 26, 2026) signaling capital return capacity
Further MSC earnings momentum as a Straits Trading subsidiary
risks: Tin price volatility if the current rally reverses
Foreign tradability/IBKR access risk for Singapore-listed S20.SI
Concentration of upside in a single subsidiary (MSC) within the broader Straits Trading structure

analyst.note: No explicit sell-side rating found; note IBKR foreign-listing tradability risk for S20.SI

evidence.note: Malaysia Smelting Corp (Straits Trading subsidiary) Q1 FY2026 net profit +5x to RM42.93M on tin prices +36% YoY; special dividend of 4 sen/share declared
